title: RFE: filter out `<none>` images in buildah images
body:
After some testing work, it produces many `<none>` images.
How about filtering out these `<none>` images and just show these images with a name, and add `--all` to list all of them.

```
# buildah images 
IMAGE ID             IMAGE NAME                                               CREATED AT             SIZE
3f8a4339aadd         docker.io/library/nginx:latest                           Dec 26, 2017 18:17     107 MB
2ececedaea32         <none>                                                   Jan 24, 2018 02:46     228.5 MB
bb7b10df1597         <none>                                                   Jan 24, 2018 02:47     228.5 MB
5e0cf9870dd4         localhost:5000/commit/nginx:latest                       Jan 24, 2018 02:47     228.5 MB
d254289a56eb         <none>                                                   Jan 24, 2018 02:48     4.199 KB
1075f9e86d86         <none>                                                   Jan 24, 2018 02:48     4.199 KB
```

comments.body:
Basically you can use `--filter` for that job. Just type:
```
# buildah images --filter "dangling=false"
```

@TomSweeneyRedHat @rhatdan WDYT

Tat is fine or just use a pipe with grep.
We should probably add a
buildah rmi --dangling 
option

This feature was implemented in #418.
Thanks @artmello & @gouyang
